Remove git push from backup-monitor.ts hook and backup.md agent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Storybook controls: pass args to render functions, hide auto-detected children control

- Updated ~30 Default stories from `render: () =>` to `render: (args) =>` and
  spread args onto primary component so controls actually update the preview
- Fixed AlertDialog to use uncontrolled mode (no useState) so defaultOpen/modal
  args are usable via controls
- Added global `argTypes: { children: { control: false } }` in preview.ts to
  suppress auto-detected children textarea on composite stories where JSX
  children always override — stories that legitimately use children as text
  (Button, Badge, Heading, etc.) retain their explicit `control: 'text'` override
- Changed Alert's explicit `children: text` to `control: false` since its render
  always uses AlertTitle + AlertDescription JSX

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: VolveStorybook
repository.description: Storybook showcase of Volve UI components

commit.message:
Fix scanner stuck in hold after upload; skip empty trunked system slots

Two fixes:
- After EPG, send KEY,S to put scanner into scan mode. Without this the
  scanner sits on whatever channel it last touched during programming and
  appears frozen (no scanning).
- Skip trunked systems (Motorola, EDACS, P25, LTR) entirely instead of
  creating an empty CSY/SIN slot with no trunk frequencies. An empty trunked
  system slot confuses the scanner's scan sequence; logging makes it clear
  why the system was skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix header buttons invisible on Android by adding html overflow-x:hidden

Root cause: decorative .curve elements (up to 881px wide) expanded
window.innerWidth to 473px on a 360px viewport in Android Chrome.
body { overflow-x: hidden } clipped visually but didn't prevent
viewport expansion, pushing position:fixed buttons off-screen.

Fix: add html { overflow-x: hidden } which properly constrains the
viewport width. Also reverted unnecessary padding-right/max-width
workarounds on .language-switcher since they're no longer needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial WinKVM project — C#/WinUI 3 port of SwiftKVM

Full port of SwiftKVM (Swift/Metal/macOS) to Windows:
- Direct3D 11 GPU rendering via SwapChainPanel (replaces Metal)
- HLSL shaders: YCbCr→RGB (pixel), HextileFill (compute), ICTDequant (compute)
- Windows.Media.Ocr — NPU-accelerated OCR on Copilot+ PCs
- e-RIC protocol over SslStream (identical logic to SwiftKVM)
- ICT decoder (Huffman + IDCT), Hextile decoder with GPU-direct fills
- AI agent: Claude, OpenAI, Grok, Ollama providers
- WinUI 3 views: Login, Main, AIChatPanel, SendText
- TOFU certificate store, profile store, AI key store

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v11.1: Cash Deployment Engine — put idle USDC to work

When USDC exceeds 50% of portfolio, the bot enters deployment mode:
- Lowers confluence buy threshold by 10 points for easier entries
- Deploys up to 30% of excess cash per cycle across up to 4 tokens
- Prioritizes underweight sectors to bring portfolio toward targets
- Keeps $200 minimum USDC reserve for gas and emergencies
- AI prompt gets explicit deployment instructions with budget targets

Fixes structural underinvestment where the bot harvested profits
aggressively but never redeployed, causing 90%+ cash positions.

commit.message:
fix: resolve playwright path error in compiled binary

bun build --compile bakes the build machine's absolute path for any
require.resolve() calls inside bundled packages. Playwright calls
require.resolve('playwright-core/package.json') internally, so on any
machine other than the CI runner it fails with "Cannot find module
D:\a\notebookllm-ts\...".

Fix:
- Mark playwright and playwright-core as --external so they are not bundled
- Add playwright-loader.ts: on first login, auto-installs playwright to
  ~/.notebookllm-ts/node_modules/ via npm, then loads it using createRequire
  from that dir (so Node module resolution starts from our managed location)
- Also auto-installs the Chromium browser via the managed playwright install
- Update release notes: npm must be available, everything else is automatic

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: HTML string regex approach for structured text extraction

Replace cheerio DOM manipulation (unreliable with non-HTML chars) with
direct regex on the raw HTML string: block tags → newlines, strip tags,
decode entities. Reliable and gives the AI properly structured text.
